meanings.definition: the act of winding or twisting
examples: he put the key in the old clock and gave it a good wind

meanings.definition: to move or cause to move in a sinuous, spiral, or circular course
examples: the river winds through the hills
the path meanders through the vineyards
sometimes, the gout wanders through the entire body
The road winds around the lake
the path twisted through the forest
roll your hair around your finger
Twine the thread around the spool
She wrapped her arms around the child

meanings.definition: catch the scent of; get wind of
examples: The dog nosed out the drugs

meanings.definition: coil the spring of (some mechanical device) by turning a stem
raise or haul up with or as if with mechanical help
examples: hoist the bicycle onto the roof of the car

meanings.definition: marked by repeated turns and bends
examples: a tortuous road up the mountain
winding roads are full of surprises
had to steer the car down a twisty track
the river followed its wandering course
